ground_truth.title: Best Business Certification Programs for Career Advancement
ground_truth.author: Sean Peek
ground_truth.main_content: A business certification can make your skills stand out and help you get the right salary. The Association for Financial Professionals reported that 87 percent of executives, 81 percent of supervisors and 90 percent of human resources professionals believe that individuals with certifications bring value to the workplace and are considered better performers than those without certifications. This perceived value can result in higher salaries, improved benefits and better career opportunities. If you're looking for the right business certification to take your career to the next level, we've outlined some popular options and their respective demand from hiring employers. Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM) is ideal for up-and-coming project managers looking to establish credibility and advance their earning potential by up to 25 percent more than non-certified professionals. Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP) is an advanced certification aimed at managers and leaders, with strong market demand reflected in over 3,200 job postings. Certified Supply Chain Professional (CSCP) holders specifically earn 36 percent higher salaries according to ASCM's 2023 supply chain salary report.
with: A business certification can make your skills stand out and help you get the right salary.
The PMP from PMI is among the most recognized and sought-after certifications for both business and information technology.
The CBAP is an advanced certification aimed at managers and leaders, with strong market demand reflected in over 3,200 job postings as of Q1 2025.
APICS-certified supply chain professionals earn median salaries of $100,000 – 27 percent greater than those with no certification.
Over 150,000 companies worldwide use Salesforce, making certified administrators highly sought-after professionals in today's job market.
without: Your free business.com+ membership unlocks exclusive tech deals and advisor support
Business.com earns commissions from some listed providers
